metadata.description: Neuro-symbolic reasoning datasets: ProofWriter, CLUTRR (mini demo subset)

examples.input:
Theory: Gary is furry. Gary is nice. Gary is red. Gary is rough. Gary is not smart. Gary is white. Gary is young. If Gary is nice and Gary is not white then Gary is red. If someone is white then they are red. All young people are furry. If someone is white and not red then they are furry. Smart, red people are rough. If Gary is not red and Gary is not furry then Gary is not smart. If Gary is white then Gary is not smart. If someone is rough and not white then they are not smart.
Query: Gary is white.
examples.metadata_all_proofs: @0: Gary is furry.[(triple1 OR ((triple7) -> rule3))] Gary is nice.[(triple2)] Gary is not smart.[(triple5 OR ((triple6) -> rule7))] Gary is red.[(triple3 OR ((triple6) -> rule2))] Gary is rough.[(triple4)] Gary is white.[(triple6)] Gary is young.[(triple7)]

examples.input:
Theory: Bob is furry. Dave is quiet. Harry is big. Rough things are white. All rough things are white. If Dave is rough then Dave is green.
Query: Bob is furry.
examples.metadata_all_proofs: @0: Bob is furry.[(triple1)] Dave is quiet.[(triple2)] Harry is big.[(triple3)]

examples.input:
Story: [Clarence]'s granddaughter, [Emily], was busy helping her brother, [Michael], move to college.
Query: ('Clarence', 'Michael')

Story: [Tony] and his sister, [June], went to a sneak preview of the Avengers movie and loved it. [Clarence] has 3 children, and one grandson. The Grandsons name is [Tony]
Query: ('Clarence', 'June')
